description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a camouflage green and brown exterior, designed for urban or tactical operations. The fuselage is angular and robust, featuring a prominent orange cockpit section at the front, which is likely armored and provides high visibility. The main rotor consists of four long, black blades mounted on a green hub, while the tail section is equipped with a vertical stabilizer and a tail rotor for directional control. The landing gear comprises two sturdy skids, reinforced with crossbars for stability during landing on uneven surfaces. The cockpit windows are large, slightly tinted, and wrap around the front, offering wide visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. No visible registration numbers or alphanumeric markings are present on the exterior.
description: A camouflaged, military-style helicopter with an orange cockpit, black main rotor, and sturdy landing skids. The design features angular lines, large cockpit windows, and a tail rotor for maneuverability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the orange cockpit, wide landing skids, and the main rotor blades extending horizontally.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the camouflage body, elongated tail, and the full length of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Right side view with clear visibility of the cockpit, main rotor, and the tail rotor assembly.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight, black main rotor blades with a slight taper, made of composite material, attached to a green central hub. No visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present, mounted vertically on the tail fin, with two black blades. No visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, black landing skids made of metal or composite, reinforced with crossbars and supports, designed for stability on various surfaces.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Large, wraparound cockpit windows at the front, slightly tinted, with black framing. The windows provide wide visibility and are divided into multiple panels.
